RULING:
FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

At the Johnston (#274950) hearing the Court granted parties' request to also argue the same motions in the Lujan (#275496), Hanson (#275624), Liberty (#275871), Turner (#275910), and Harig (#276181) cases. Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as to the Turner case: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on September 21, 2012 is set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on February 1, 2016 is set aside pursuant to C.C.P. 473(b) as well as the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(b). Trial is November 4, 2013 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. The last day of trial is November 7, 2013. Before any motions in limine are filed with the court the defendants are ordered to meet and confer on all motions in limine in order to avoid frivolous and duplicative hearings. If the parties do not meet and confer on the motions in limine the court will take appropriate actions. Last day for hearing summary motions is October 31, 2013. Summary motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant sections of the C.C.P., unless plaintiffs stipulate to shortened notice at the hearing. The time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. Deloris Turner's deposition must commence and conclude no later than July 26, 2013. Plaintiffs must return all outstanding authorizations to Berry & Berry within 7 days of this hearing. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than August 9, 2013. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 16, 2013. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on October 24, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is October 18, 2013. Expert discovery cut-off date is November 1, 2013. No later than July 26, 2013, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than July 26, 2013, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further C. M. C. on September 18, 2013 at 1:30pm. By September 16, 2013, the parties must notify the court in writing to confirm the need for the C. M. C. If the court does not hear from the parties, the case management conference will go off calendar. If there is a C. M. C., the parties may appear at by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to S. F. Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on November 4, 2013 at 1:30pm in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by October 31, 2013 at 2:00pm. The expert witness disclosures are due 50 days before the trial and expert pare down list is due 30 days before trial. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
